body.body:
Surfaced during T-test-data-lifecycle sprint 2026-04-15. goal_graph._store_memory does a raw INSERT INTO memories via cortex._conn() instead of cortex.store(). This violates the single-chokepoint principle from CLAUDE.md (DP4: all r/w through Cortex only). It also means any metadata-stamping done at cortex.store() level (like the new IGOR_TEST_MODE=1 test-data tagging) is bypassed.

## Why it matters

cortex.store() is the single place where per-memory stamps like test_data, scrub, credential leakage filtering happen. Anything bypassing it is an untagged hole in those systems.

Current workaround (committed in T-test-data-lifecycle): manual stamp_metadata_for_test_mode call inside _store_memory. This works but duplicates the logic that should live in one place.

## Scope

1. Convert goal_graph._store_memory to construct a Memory object with memory_type=REFERENCE + narrative + metadata, then call cortex.store()
2. Remove the manual test-data stamp workaround (it becomes redundant)
3. Check _store_metadata (the UPDATE path) — may need a similar conversion if it's still raw
4. Grep for other `cortex._conn()` direct writes in tools/ and file separate tickets if found

## Tests

- Existing test_goal_graph tests should pass unchanged
- Verify test_data tag applies automatically (no manual stamp path needed)
- Scrub still applies (it was applied by the raw-INSERT path via earlier logic? verify)

## Follow-up

A broader audit: find every direct cortex._conn() INSERT in tools/ — each one is a potential bypass. Could be a separate S ticket or an audit check registered with audit_add.
body.title: goal_graph._store_memory bypasses cortex.store — convert to single chokepoint
body.result: Converted _store_memory to cortex.store() via Memory(REFERENCE). Auto-gains scrub + test_data stamping from single chokepoint. Removed duplicated stamp_metadata_for_test_mode workaround. 18 goal_graph tests + 2484 total pass. Runtime bypass audit filed as T-cortex-store-bypass-audit.
